title: Responsive Design — Media Queries
description: Make your blog theme responsive so it looks great on phones, tablets, and desktops. Add @media queries to stack the CSS Grid layout to a single column below 768px, switch the nav from a row to a column layout on small screens, and reduce font sizes for mobile readability.
test_code:
assert '@media' in css.lower(), 'Missing @media query'
assert 'max-width' in css.lower() or '768' in css, 'Media query should target a breakpoint (e.g., 768px)'
assert css.lower().count('grid-template-columns') >= 2, 'Need grid-template-columns: 1fr inside media query to stack to one column'
assert '1fr' in css.lower(), 'Use grid-template-columns: 1fr to stack to a single column on small screens'
acceptance_criteria: Grid layout stacks to a single column (grid-template-columns: 1fr)
Navigation switches to a vertical column layout on small screens
Font sizes are adjusted for mobile readability
hints: Inside the media query, set .page-grid { grid-template-columns: 1fr; }
Change .nav to flex-direction: column for a vertical mobile menu
